name: La Tenace — loop in the Gers
name_original: La Tenace — boucle dans le Gers
region_intro: Road cycling routes around L'Isle-Jourdain offer varied landscapes, from the flat Garonne terraces to the rolling hills of Gascony, providing diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features an extensive network of well-paved surfaces, suitable for road bikes of all types. Cyclists can explore areas with elevations reaching up to 340 meters, offering panoramic views, or enjoy routes along river systems and around the Lac de l'Isle-Jourdain. The area's topography allows for both gentle rides and more challenging excursions with significant elevation gains. Best road cycling routes around L'Isle-Jourdain The most popular road cycling route is La Tenace — loop in the Gers, a 77.7 miles (125.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 46 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the varied landscapes of the Gers region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View from Castéra – Garac Viewpoint loop from L'Isle-Jourdain, a moderate 29.8 miles (48.0 km) path. This route offers views from Castéra and the Garac viewpoint, showcasing the local scenery. Local road cyclists also love the L'Isle-Jourdain – L'Isle-Jourdain Town Center loop from L'Isle-Jourdain, a 14.6 miles (23.5 km) trail leading through the town center and surrounding areas, often completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes. Road cycling around L'Isle-Jourdain is defined by rolling hills, flat Garonne terraces, and the impressive Viaduct of l'Isle-Jourdain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to challenging rides with notable climbs. The routes in L'Isle-Jourdain are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 38 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore L'Isle-Jourdain's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around L'Isle-Jourdain?
A: The region offers a diverse range of landscapes for road cyclists. You'll find everything from the flat Garonne terraces to the rolling hills of Gascony, with some areas reaching elevations of up to 340 meters. This variety allows for both gentle rides and more challenging excursions with significant elevation gains.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the L'Isle-Jourdain area?
A: There are over 160 road cycling routes around L'Isle-Jourdain, catering to various skill levels. This includes 13 easy routes, 80 moderate routes, and 69 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or casual rides?
A: Yes, L'Isle-Jourdain offers several easy routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35859942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Isle-Jourdain – L'Isle-Jourdain Town Center loop from L'Isle-Jourdain&lt;/a&gt;, a 23.5 km trail that takes you through the town center and surrounding areas, perfect for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging long-distance road cycling routes in the region?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1276378509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tenace — loop in the Gers&lt;/a&gt;, a 125 km route traversing the Gers region, or the 129 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15111246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Samatan Covered Market – Bastide and Cologne hall loop from L'Isle-Jourdain&lt;/a&gt;, offer significant distances and elevation gains.

Q: What notable landmarks or natural features can I see while cycling around L'Isle-Jourdain?
A: Cyclists can enjoy several points of interest. The impressive 304-meter-long Viaduct of l'Isle-Jourdain is a prominent landmark. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5174963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bouconne Forest&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the recreational &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3124667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordette Lake&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/155744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Castéra&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic vistas.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, many routes in the rolling hills of Gascony provide panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36848227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Castéra – Garac Viewpoint loop from L'Isle-Jourdain&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 48 km path specifically designed to showcase local scenery and viewpoints.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in L'Isle-Jourdain?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 38 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from flat sections to rolling hills, and the well-paved surfaces that make for excellent road biking.

Q: Can I incorporate cultural or historical sites into my road cycling trips?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in heritage. You can cycle past historical landmarks like the Saint-Martin Abbatial Church, or visit the European Museum of Campanaire Art. Some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15111246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Samatan Covered Market – Bastide and Cologne hall loop from L'Isle-Jourdain&lt;/a&gt;, take you through areas with cultural significance like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1437059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastide and Cologne hall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in L'Isle-Jourdain?
A: L'Isle-Jourdain benefits from a temperate climate, making it suitable for year-round outdoor activities. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, with less extreme heat than summer and fewer cold days than winter.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes around L'Isle-Jourdain are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28949678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Isle-Jourdain Town Center – Samatan Covered Market loop from L'Isle-Jourdain&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 81.8 km ride, and the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35859942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Isle-Jourdain – L'Isle-Jourdain Town Center loop from L'Isle-Jourdain&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is L'Isle-Jourdain accessible by public transport for cyclists?
A: L'Isle-Jourdain is a designated 'Gare Étape' (station stop), indicating its accessibility by rail. For specific information on train services and carrying bikes, you can check the details on the &lt;a href="https://www.visit-occitanie.com/en/rail-tour/gares-etapes/lisle-jourdain/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit Occitanie website&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Samatan is famous for its duck and poultry market. You will find many cafes and restaurants there for a city of this size.
The city center, where most shops and services are located, is now eligible for community funding for its renovation.
Small town in Toulouse Gascony where you can discover the European Museum of Campanaire Art under the old grain market, the Claude Augé house, or the Saint-Martin collegiate church.
